title: trim opcode — stack frame trimming in tail calls
purpose: The `trim` opcode (opcode 136) removes N words from the top of the current stack frame's Y-register slots while keeping the remaining slots alive. The compiler emits `trim` before tail calls when some Y-registers are dead but the frame cannot be fully deallocated yet (e.g., a try/catch is still active). Without `trim`, any compiled code using this optimization hits `UnsupportedOpcode(136)`. This is common in OTP 25+ compiled code with nested function calls inside try/catch blocks.
task: Wire the existing `Instruction::Trim { words, remaining }` variant (already decoded by the loader) into the opcode dispatch table and implement the handler in `core.rs`.

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch wiring
requirements.spec: Add `Instruction::Trim` to the dispatch table in `opcodes/mod.rs`, routing to a handler in `core.rs`.
acceptance: `Instruction::Trim { words, remaining }` is matched in the dispatch table
No `UnsupportedOpcode(136)` error when executing modules containing `trim`

requirements.title: trim handler implementation
requirements.spec: Implement `trim` in `core.rs`. The instruction removes `words` Y-register slots from the current frame, keeping `remaining` live slots. The live slots are the lowest-numbered Y-registers (Y0, Y1, ..., Y_{remaining-1}).
acceptance: `trim(process, words, remaining)` shrinks the current frame's Y-register slots
After trim, the frame has exactly `remaining` Y-register slots
The kept slots retain their values (Y0..Y_{remaining-1} are unchanged)
`words + remaining` SHALL equal the frame's current Y-slot count (badarg otherwise)
Test: allocate frame with 5 Y-regs, set Y0=10, Y1=20, Y2=30, Y3=40, Y4=50. trim(2, 3). Frame now has 3 Y-regs: Y0=10, Y1=20, Y2=30.
Test: trim(0, N) is a no-op — frame unchanged
Test: trim on empty stack raises an error

requirements.title: Stack frame y_regs truncation
requirements.spec: Add a method to `StackFrame` (or use direct Vec truncation) that supports removing the highest-numbered Y-register slots.
acceptance: `y_slots` field is updated to match the new count
Test: unit test on StackFrame directly verifying slot count and values after trim

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the stack frame structure — trim operates within the existing `StackFrame::y_regs` Vec
SHALL NOT deallocate the frame — trim only shrinks it; deallocate is a separate instruction
SHALL NOT touch the return address or any other frame metadata — only Y-register slots are affected
Note: core.rs is at 583 lines (over 500-line guideline). trim adds ~20 lines. Full core.rs split is a separate concern.
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
